title: Shot Logic Foundation
objective: Tighten the cut-reason system, expand the information DAG model to handle branching dependencies, multiple information channels, and temporal constraints. Make every rule in shot-logic.md falsifiable and computable.
storyQuestions: Can the 3 cut-reasons (information/reaction/emphasis) explain every cut in Mirror Visitor ch1, or do we need a 4th?
Does the DAG model handle simultaneous PROVIDES (a single shot providing info on two channels)?
When two shots both satisfy a REQUIRES, how does the DAG distinguish canonical provider vs redundant?
Can the VERIFY gate be expressed as computable predicates that an LLM can execute without ambiguity?
baseAcceptanceChecks: Every cut-reason rule has a predicate form (IF condition THEN reason)
The DAG model handles multi-channel PROVIDES
The VERIFY gate has a numbered predicate checklist
At least one new cut-reason edge case documented with resolution
